_risk_level_doc: Two-file change; each file adds one local variable declaration and substitutes two literal values. No logic branch added or removed. Default value identical to current hardcoded value.

requirement.original: MICRO fix: parameterize hardcoded flock timeout constants in two scripts that were flagged by the style inspector as Standard 2/9 violations (introduced_in_diff: true) blocking close of task 20260525-050824.
requirement.clarified: In scripts/score-inject.sh and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh: add LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" near the flock call site, replace the bare integer 10 in each flock command with "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}", and update adjacent stderr messages from 'within 10s' to 'within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s'.
requirement.what: Replace two hardcoded flock timeout integer literals with a named LOCK_TIMEOUT shell variable
requirement.why: Standard 2/9 (no-hardcoded-values) violations introduced in diff for task 20260525-050824 are blocking close of that task

included: scripts/score-inject.sh lines 64-65: flock call and adjacent stderr message
scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh lines 52-53: flock call and adjacent stderr message
excluded: scripts/score-update.sh (pre-existing hardcode assessed as false positive, provision α)

success_criteria: bash -n syntax check exits 0 for both scripts
LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" declaration present before flock call in both scripts
No bare flock -s -w 10 or flock -w 10 remaining in either script
Adjacent stderr messages use ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s not hardcoded 10s
constraints: Do not modify any file other than the two named scripts
Do not add CLI flags, validation, or new config files
LOCK_TIMEOUT must use env-override form: LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"
Always pass as "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" (double-quoted) in flock call

out_of_scope_observations.observation: flock -w 10 9 — same pattern as the genuine violations, but assessed as false positive (pre-existing line not in diff's changed-line range; provision α of AC-2.6(b) applies). Not required for this cycle's close.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Style inspector flags Standard 2/9 violations for flock timeout hardcoding in two scripts introduced in Cycle 5 diff, blocking close of task 20260525-050824
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Cycle 5 M2 created scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh with flock -w 10; Cycle 5 M3 added a shared flock block to scripts/score-inject.sh with flock -s -w 10. Both used a bare integer literal rather than a named constant.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — inferred from spec section 2 Cycle 5 M2/M3 description and confirmed by direct Read of the two files
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Flock timeout of 10 seconds was chosen as a reasonable default; the integer was written inline without parameterization
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Standard 2/9 (no-hardcoded-values) requires named constants for numeric literals; inline integers fail the style inspector and block close
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Introduced in task 20260525-050824 (Cycle 5); detected by style inspector in close attempt; confirmed genuine violations in spec Section 8 Cycle 7
observations: flock -s -w 10 8 at scripts/score-inject.sh:64 (inferred: Read tool result)
echo '...within 10s' at scripts/score-inject.sh:65 (inferred: Read tool result)
flock -w 10 9 at scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh:52 (inferred: Read tool result)
echo '...within 10s' at scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh:53 (inferred: Read tool result)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime execution; style violation is a static text pattern, runtime observation not applicable)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Direct Read of both scripts at lines specified in the source spec confirmed the bare integer literals

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression bug — new code introduced in Cycle 5 diff; no bisect needed

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: replace flock -s -w 10 with flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" in score-inject.sh, add LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" declaration
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: scripts/score-inject.sh contains LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" before the flock call and uses flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 8 at the call site

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: replace flock -w 10 with flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" in lifecycle-baseline-import.sh, add LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" declaration
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh contains LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" before the flock call and uses flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 9 at the call site

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Update any adjacent stderr message referencing '10s' to use ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Neither target script contains the string 'within 10s'; both contain 'within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s' in their lock-failure stderr messages

component_chain.role: Acquires shared flock on lifecycle.jsonl.lock before reading lifecycle JSONL; timeout hardcoded at line 64
Acquires exclusive flock on lifecycle.jsonl.lock before appending baseline import entries; timeout hardcoded at line 52

setup.reason: non-UI -- config; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints)

evidence.observed: Style inspector report for task 20260525-050824 flagged flock -s -w 10 and flock -w 10 as Standard 2/9 violations with introduced_in_diff: true

measured.value: flock -s -w 10 8 at score-inject.sh:64; flock -w 10 9 at lifecycle-baseline-import.sh:52; both adjacent stderr messages contain 'within 10s'

expected.value: flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 8 and flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 9 with LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" declaration; stderr messages use ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s

evidence.gap: Two bare integer literals 10 in newly introduced flock calls; two adjacent stderr strings hardcode 10s

all_occurrences: scripts/score-update.sh:76 (out of scope — pre-existing false positive)

reference_source.description: spec-20260520-221059.md Section 7 Cycle 7 verbatim instructions; direct Read of affected scripts confirmed the literal values

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts for this specific MICRO fix

context.codebase_state: master branch, working tree dirty with staged and unstaged changes from task 20260525-050824 cycle
context.recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification

development_approach.strategy: For each target script: (1) insert LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" on a new line before the flock call; (2) replace the bare integer 10 in the flock command with "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}"; (3) replace 'within 10s' in the adjacent stderr message with 'within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s'. Minimal Edit operations only.
development_approach.validation_approach: bash -n syntax check on both scripts; grep-based AC verification per acceptance-criteria-20260526-042454.json

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed MICRO tier is correct. Flagged 7 issues: (1) score-update.sh optional mention is scope creep contradicting non-goals, (2) non-goals self-contradiction, (3) AC2 too weak (comment/wrong-location false pass), (4) AC3 line-number brittle after insertion shifts lines, (5) AC4 absence-only weaker than positive assertion, (6) bash expansion word-split risk needs explicit note, (7) 'parameterize' wording could imply over-engineering. All 7 had PROPOSED_FIX.

findings.issue: Optional score-update.sh mention is scope creep and contradicts non-goal 'Do NOT modify any other scripts'
findings.proposed_fix: Affected files: scripts/score-inject.sh and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh only. scripts/score-update.sh is out of scope for this MICRO change and must not be modified.
findings.rationale: score-update.sh was removed from affected files entirely; non-goals updated to name it explicitly

findings.issue: Non-goals contradict affected-file scope by listing 'optionally score-update.sh' while also saying 'Do NOT modify any other scripts'
findings.proposed_fix: Do NOT modify test files, acceptance criteria files, settings.json, scripts/score-update.sh, or any file other than the two named scripts.
findings.rationale: Non-goals updated to name score-update.sh explicitly as forbidden

findings.issue: AC2 too weak — 'grep confirms LOCK_TIMEOUT variable present' could pass for a comment, unused assignment, or wrong location
findings.proposed_fix: Each target script declares exactly one LOCK_TIMEOUT assignment near the lock call, before the flock invocation
findings.rationale: AC2 updated to check for the exact assignment form and that it precedes the flock call

findings.issue: AC3 is line-number brittle — adding a declaration shifts original line numbers
findings.proposed_fix: Verify command shape: score-inject.sh uses flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 8; lifecycle-baseline-import.sh uses flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 9; neither contains flock -s -w 10 or flock -w 10
findings.rationale: AC3 rewritten as command-shape check with absence grep

findings.issue: AC4 absence-only check is weaker than confirming the desired stderr text
findings.proposed_fix: AC4: Adjacent messages include within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s AND no longer include within 10s
findings.rationale: AC4 now asserts both absence of 10s and presence of ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s

findings.issue: LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" permits external env override and must stay quoted in flock call to avoid word splitting
findings.proposed_fix: Implementation note: env-override is intended; always pass as "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}"; do not add CLI flags or validation
findings.rationale: Added implementation note to spec and constraint in context JSON; env-override form is the mandated pattern per spec

findings.issue: Parameterize wording could imply adding CLI options, config files, or tests
findings.proposed_fix: Non-goal: Do not add CLI options, new config files, validation helpers, tests, or documentation updates.
findings.rationale: Non-goals expanded to explicitly forbid those over-engineering vectors

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 7 Codex findings applied: score-update.sh removed from scope entirely, non-goals made explicit, AC2-AC4 tightened, implementation note added for bash quoting

research_findings.reason: MICRO tier bug fix with clear root cause from spec; no research needed
